page: Google Cloud Platform e2-small is a shared-core virtual machine instance belonging to the E2 instance family. It features 2 vCPUs allocated from a shared AMD EPYC 7B12 physical processor using the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 1 physical core, 2 threads per core, and a fractional CPU allocation of one-quarter of a physical core. It includes 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core, and does not bundle local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations place the e2-small in the lower performance tier for most compute and memory operations. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Passmark CPU and memory scores, rank in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is limited, with read-only large block speeds at 2.47 GB/sec and write speeds at 2.26 GB/sec. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 24.74 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are modest, yielding 0.18 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 0.54 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

Due to its shared-core architecture and low memory capacity, the e2-small is designed for low-intensity workloads where resource density and cost efficiency are prioritized over raw performance. The lack of dedicated CPU cores and local storage limits its suitability for heavy database operations or complex data processing. It is best suited for lightweight applications, microservices, development or testing environments, and basic web serving tasks that do not require sustained high CPU utilization.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-small is a shared-core virtual machine instance designed for entry-level workloads. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 vCPUs with a fractional allocation of one-quarter of a physical core, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, generally fall within the bottom 10%, though single-threaded gzip compression achieves average tier results. With limited memory bandwidth and a shared CPU resource model, the e2-small offers a highly cost-efficient option for low-demand applications. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as microservices, development environments, and basic web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-small is a shared-core instance with 2 vCPUs and 2 GB RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 7B12, it is a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads, development environments, and basic web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-small is a shared-core instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 2 GB RAM, designed for budget-friendly, low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine designed for lightweight development environments, microservices, and low-intensity background tasks.
bullet_points: Shared-core architecture utilizing AMD EPYC 7B12 processors
2 vCPUs with a fractional physical core allocation and 2.0 GB RAM
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average performance in single-threaded gzip compression tasks
Highly cost-efficient profile for non-production and testing environments
Best suited for lightweight microservices and basic web serving
